finding: Lymphomas are a heterogeneous group of neoplasms with varying sites of GI involvement + varying

differentials.name: Stage I  tumor confined to bowel wall
differentials.raw: Stage I  tumor confined to bowel wall

differentials.name: Stage II  limited to local nodes
differentials.raw: Stage II  limited to local nodes

differentials.name: abdominal pain, weight loss Location:   10-25% of NHL are extranodal; stomach (50%) > small bowel > ileocecal region (← Peyer patches) > colon + rectum (0.4%) > esophagus; multicentric in 10-50% Site:   originates in submucosa / deep mucosal layer → may be missed at endoscopy
differentials.raw: abdominal pain, weight loss Location:   10-25% of NHL are extranodal; stomach (50%) > small bowel > ileocecal region (← Peyer patches) > colon + rectum (0.4%) > esophagus; multicentric in 10-50% Site:   originates in submucosa / deep mucosal layer → may be missed at endoscopy

differentials.name: Inherited polyposis
differentials.raw: Inherited polyposis (family history, in childhood / early adulthood, mucocutaneous pigmentation, fibromatosis, osteomas, dental anomalies)

differentials.name: Blood-borne metastases: breast, lung, malignant melanoma, lymphoma
differentials.raw: Blood-borne metastases: breast, lung, malignant melanoma, lymphoma (usually larger lesions, more sporadic, submucosal masses, bull's-eye / target appearance with central ulcer)

context: gross and histologic features → wide spectrum of imaging findings Radiographic morphology: √  enlarged nodular folds with a range of nodule sizes √  solitary / multiple smooth subepithelial polypoid masses that may ulcerate √  cobblestone pattern ← lymphomatous polyps √  normal / thickened surrounding tissue √  sprue pattern → may cause intussusception √  multiple lymphomatous polyposis =  rare disorder characterized by numerous small polypoid lesions covering long segments of GI tract, typically a manifestation of mantle cell lymphoma √  circumferential bulky mass in intestinal wall, often ex
